question_text: All officers must take the confirmation examination within...........years from the date of assuming duty.
review_notes: Answer key entry for question 1 was malformed as "I. d" and was normalized to "1. D".

question_text: Where Gazette of 1st Appointment and Confirmation of Appointment are not ready within.........years, COMPRO examination result should be used in their place.
The emolument of newly employed officers shall be paid as from the date of assumption of duty and they shall be placed on IPPIS platform within.......months of assumption of duty.
If an Officer is promoted to a grade level that does not overlap his previous grade level, he will be placed at the......of his new salary grade level.
Payment of promotion arrears would be made within the.......promotion is effected.
An Officer on an incremental grade level would normally be granted an increment unless he is on:
option_c: his MDA's reward and regonition list.

question_text: The Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER) was replaced with.....................
The Performance Management System (PMS) focuses on..............
option_a: employees' measurable output and certain competencies required to deliver job tasks and meet agreed targets.
option_b: making incongruent the organizational goals and individual employee objectives
option_c: coercing the immediate supervisor, peers, reporting officer and countersigning officer involved in the employee appraisal circle.
option_d: encouraging a retribution in the perfomance appraisal results.

question_text: A Reporting Officer in respect of the Performance Management System must be:
option_a: At least a substantive Director on Grade Level 17.
option_b: At most a senior officer of commendable attribues.
option_c: An immediate Supervisor and at least one substantive grade level above the Appraisee Officer.
option_d: At most an officer of a Directorate cadre in the Human Resources Management Department where the Officer being reported on works.

question_text: An Officer on secondment must be appraised by:
option_a: the Office that the officer is seconded to.
option_b: the releasing MDA that the Officer originally belongs.
option_c: the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

question_text: Perfomance Management Cycle will run...............
option_a: from April to March of every year.
option_b: from July to June of every year.
option_c: from October to September of every year.
option_d: from January to December of every year.

question_text: Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) should be drawn and agreed upon by.......... and final appraisal should be done by.............
option_a: March of each year, September of the same year.
option_b: April of each year, October of the same year.
option_c: January of each year, December of the same year.
option_d: May of each year, November of the same year.

question_text: In a situation where an Appraisee's performance falls below expectations (under-performance),.............will be adopted.
Appraisal of officers must be conducted.............
option_d: in the presence of senior Directors.

question_text: An Officer who is authorized to attend a course of instruction outside Nigeria must sign a Bond to refund to Government all expenses for non-compliance with the following except:
option_a: obtain a certificate of satisfactory attendance at the course.
option_c: remain in Service within a period of three years of his completing such a course.
option_d: undertake any other course of instruction without the specific approval of the Government.

question_text: A senior Officer sent overseas on a course of instruction for than.......months will be eligible for free air passage for himself and his spouse.
Officers may be granted study leave with pay subject to a maximum of......years.
For a journey by air,............. is for Permanent Secretaries, Ministers and above, while.......... is for officers on GL 7-17
The Public Service Rules are applicable to which Officers?
option_b: Judicial Officers of Courts of Record.
option_c: Police Service Commission officers and the Officers of the Nigerian Army.
option_d: All officers in the Public Service except where the rules conflict with specific terms approved by the Federal Government and written into the contract of employement or letters of appointment.

question_text: The following organs of Government of the Federation derive their appointments from the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria except:
option_a: The Nigerian President, Vice-President, President of the Senate and Members, Speaker of the House of Representatives and Members, Secretary to the Government of the Federation and other Members of the Federal Executive Council
option_b: Chief Justice of Nigeria, Justices of the Supreme Court, President and Justices of the COurt of Appeal, Chief Judge and Judges of the High Court of the High Court of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Grand Khadis and Kahdis of the Sharia Court of Appeal of the FCT, President and Judges of the Customary Court of Appeal of the FCT.
option_c: All State Governors, Special Advicers to the State Government, Permanent Secretaries of Treasury Funded Local Governments Chairmen and their appointees.
option_d: The Chairmen and Members of the following bodies: Auditor-General for the Federation, Code of Conduct Bureau; Code of Conduct Tribunal, FCSC, FCC, INEC, NPC, PSC and RMAFC

question_text: The Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria is:
option_b: Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_d: Special Adviser to the President on Foreign Affairs.

question_text: Civil Service of the Federation is a
option_a: Parastatals of the Government of the Federation.
option_b: All staff of the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC) Plc.
option_c: Body or Organ which enjoys continuity of existence.
option_d: All the Personal Assistants to the Accounting Officers of the Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs).

question_text: Classified Correspondence is a correspondence which has been graded:
option_d: Restricted, Confidential, Secret or Top Secret.

question_text: The Appointment of junior staff on grade level 06 and below is handled by:
option_a: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation only.
option_b: Accountant General of the Federation in conjunction with the IPPIS Office.
option_c: Junior Staff Committee as approved by the Accounting Officer of a Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office.
option_d: Secretary to the Government of the Federation.

question_text: An appointment of officers of grade level 07-17 is handled by
option_b: Accountant General of the Federation in conjunction with the IPPIS Office.
option_c: Special Adviser to the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria on labour and productivity.
option_d: Secretary to the Government of the Federation.

question_text: The submission of applications for the appointment of officers of grade level 07-17 is within:
An annual competitive Public/Civil Service Entry Examination for is held for new entrants and tranferring serving officers on Grade Level:
Candidates with required qualifications who are to be appointed into the State/ Zonal Offices of a Ministry/ Extra-Ministerial Office must be:
option_a: Candidates who studied in any Higher Institution in those States/ Zones
option_c: Candidates who did their NYSC Service in those States/ Zones.
option_d: Residents of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).

question_text: The incremental date of an Officer appointed, or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service is...............on the condition that such Officer has spent at least...........on the Grade with good performance before enjoying the increment.
option_a: 31st December of every year, twelve (12) months
option_b: 1st June of every year, five (5) months
option_c: 31st March of every year, three (3) months
option_d: 1st January of every year, six (6) months

question_text: Seniority in the Public Service is based on the.................. certified by an authorized Officer and or..................as reflected in the appropriate register and career progression of the concerned Officers.
option_a: Exit date from the previous employment, date of last promotion examination
option_b: Approved leave date from the initial engagement, date of last entry examination
option_c: Date on the last pay certificate, date of previous employment pay slip.
option_d: Entry date/the date of assumption of duty, date of present appointment.

question_text: The employment of unpaid staff into the public service is..........

A Staff Record Form is also called.............., a copy of which must be sent to the National Record Centre in the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation, Accountant General's Office, Auditor General's Office and PenCom within............. of the appointment
Any change of date of birth in the record of service or other personnel records upon employment into the Public Service is regarded as..............
option_d: An act of proper use of affidavit of change of birth date.

question_text: ...............means the filling of vacancies by direct appointment of persons not already in any of the Public Service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

A person with requisite qualification can be employed into the public service as a trainee or pupil, on probation in a pensionable post, on non-pensionable contract, acting appointment and.................
option_b: Appointment based on recommendation from a High Chief or Political Head.

question_text: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC)'s employment of.......................is automatic irrespective of vacancy constraints:
option_a: Confidential Driver to the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_b: Orderly to the Honourable Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).
option_c: National Youth Service Corps. (NYSC) Presidential Merit Award Winners.
option_d: Chief Chef to the First Lady of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

question_text: .....................of all recruitments in the Service are to be reserved for.....................
option_a: 12% Confidential Driver to the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_b: 20% Orderly to the Honourable Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).
option_c: 10% Chief Chef to the First Lady of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

question_text: Eligibility for appointing every applicant into the Federal Public Service are (i) Between the age of 18 and 50 years; (ii) Having minimum qualification
option_a: s contained in the scheme of service; (iii)Having being certified by an authorized Healthcare Provider as medically fit; (iv) Having a testimonial of good conduct from the last employer or school attended; (v) Having requisite qualification as contained in the scheme of service; and
option_b: any officer seeking for an appointment into the Federal Public Service must seek clearance from the National Pension Commission.
option_c: no officer should seek Federal Government's appointment without Police extract.
option_d: every officer must possess a bank guarantee to confirm his/her loan and debt status.
review_notes: Option A label was malformed in source: "as contained in the scheme of service; (iii)Having being certified by an authorized Healthcare Provider as medically fit; (iv) Having a testimonial of good conduct from the last employer or school attended;".

Duplicate option label A found in source: "a. no officer shall be appointed into the Federal Public Service without authorized establishment and recruitment waiver from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.".

question_text: No candidate would be considered for an appointment into the Federal Public Service for the following reasons except:
option_a: he has been convicted of a criminal offence.
option_b: he has been dismissed or called upon to resign or retire from the Public Service before.
option_c: he has left his previous employment without reasons and whether he is free from financial embarrassment.
option_d: he is into agricultural value chain.

question_text: When a newly appointed Officer assumes duty, he must enter his personal particulars on..........
In spite of the Freedom of Information Act, a newly employed Officer must sign..........
Which of the membership of the following association can be regarded as an act of serious misconduct in the Public Service.
option_a: Association of Ram Seller, FCT Chapter.

question_text: Membership of Junior Staff Committee (JSC) and Senior Staff Committee (SSC) in Parastatals includes which of the following:
option_a: Representative of Department of Human Resources Management of the supervising Ministry and An observer from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
option_b: Representative of the Ministry of Labour, Productivity and Employment.
option_c: Representative of the National Directorate of employment (NDE).
option_d: Representative of the National Productivity Centre (NPC).

question_text: Kinds of short-term appointment in the Public Service include contract appointment, sabbatical appointment, career exchange programme, talent sourcing strategy, intership and
Conditions for the contract appointment include the following except.
option_a: The Public Service no longer has officers with specific skills as provided in thhe scheme of service.
option_b: The contract appointment must be tied to funded projects/programmes with specific life span.
option_c: The contract appointment can be offered to expatriates when suitable Nigerians are not available and to Nigerians who either pensioners or over 50 years of age.

question_text: Spouses married to Nigerians would remain on contract appointment, and not considered for permanent appointments until:
The contract appointment should not be below which salary grade level (SGL)?
The Contract appointment duration should be........ or.........and renewable but not more than........ years in total.
option_b: six (6) months or nine (9) months, two (2)

question_text: A contract officer must.............
option_a: accept posting or transfer to any part of the country.
option_b: refuse posting or transfer to any part of the country.
option_c: write petition to the Federal Civil Service Commission for posting or transfer outside the officer's Head office.
option_d: demand for double salary for any posting or transfer to any part of the country.

question_text: A contract officer should be subjected to.............
option_a: any valid relief from any performance evaluation.
option_b: performance appraisal through confidential report as applicable in the Public Service.
option_d: scrutiny for non-deduction of pension element from his salary.

question_text: A contract officer must sign...........
option_b: A day-on, a day-off leave notice.

question_text: A retired officer from the Public Service who is re-engaged into a career post, his appointment must be on a salary grade level immediately........that on which he retired and be eligible to maintain his.......step at retirement.
An Officer serving on contract who wants to be re-engaged must formally write through the Accounting Officer at least............ before his leave is due.
review_notes: Answer key entry had trailing punctuation in source: "53. c.".

question_text: ..............Appointment is applicable to an Officer on a salary grade level (SGL)............. for a period of 12 calendar months.
An internship is for a period of.........months on part-time while it is for a period of......months on full-time.
............ is the permanent release of an Officer from one scheduled service to another or from one class to another within the same Service.
............is the temporary release of an Officer to the service of another Government, approved body or any recognised International Organization or body for a specific period.
An Officer on transfer from State to Federal Service would be placed on a grade level........than his current grade level.

Secondment is for a maximum period of....... years in the first instance after which the Officer may apply for extension or return to his former post but the total period of such secondment must not exceed........years
A secondment in the public interest must not exceed........years.
An Officer on.................... receives salary from the MDA with the higher pay when there is a differential in payments between the releasing and receiving MDAs.
A transfer of an Officer from one scheduled service to another is subject to........................
option_a: Availability of funds to pay his salary and other allowances.
option_b: Availability of vacancies in the relevant grade and the applicant's discipline or area of specialty.
option_c: Availability of other professionals within the Officer's cadre.
option_d: Availability of adequate training programmes for the Officer's upskilling.

question_text: No Ministry/MDA is authorized to transfer or second any senior officer to any Parastatal under its supervision or accept any Officer seconded to it without the endorsement of...........
option_a: Staff Mobility Department of the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF)
option_b: Office of the Secretary to the Government of the Federation.

question_text: Posting of Officers to posts outside their Professional Cadres is.............
Posting of pool Officers in the Service becomes mandatory after every.............years.

question_text: A period of Acting appointment must not exceed........... but in exceptional circumstances may be extended to another.............
All Officers who fall within the field of selection for any promotion exercise would be considered except...............
option_a: those wish to remain in their current grade level for any fear of being posted out of the present office.
option_b: those have been advised by their clergymen to remain satisfied with the current position.
option_c: those who are under disciplinary action.
option_d: those who feel disatisfied with the recent currency devaluation.

question_text: The minimum of number of years for the eligibility of officers on GL 06 and below, GL 07-14 and GL 15-17 are............,.............. and................respectively.
option_a: Minimum of 3 years, 4 years and 5 years.
option_b: Minimum of 1 year, 2 years and 3 years.
option_c: Minimum of 4 years, 5 years and 6 years.
option_d: Minimum of 2 years, 3 years and 4 years.

question_text: Which MDAs are responsible for promotion of officers on GL 06 and below; GL 07 to 14 and GL 15-17?
option_a: Ministry, Department and Agency (MDA) via Junior Staff COmmittee, MDA or relevant staff pool with the approval of OHCSF subject to confirmation by FCSC, FCSC on recommendation from MDA or the relevant pool routed through OHCSF.
option_b: Presidency, Ministry of Labour and Productivity, Auditor General for the Federation.
option_c: Accountant General of the Federation, Staff Mobility Department of the Federal Civil Service Commission, Employee Promotion Department of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
option_d: National Productivity Centre (NPC), MDA in conjunction with the National Directorate of Employment, Presidential Initiative on Federal Government employees' performance Appraisal Policty.

question_text: The effective date of all promotions in the Public Service is.............
review_notes: Answer key entry had trailing punctuation in source: "71. d.".

question_text: The eligibility criteria for the post of permanent secretaries entails the following except:
option_a: being a Federal Civil Service Director on GL 17 for at least two years and on IPPIS.
option_b: Not retiring on or before the 31st of December of the following year.
option_c: Having a proof of indigenship of the State where vacancy exists but the indigenship must not be by marriage in case of a female Civil Servant.
option_d: Having pending disciplinary action but already seeking presidential intervention.

question_text: The examination process for the post of permanent secretaries involve:

Officers who fail promotion examinations after............consecutive attempts or who absent themselves from an examination without reasons acceptable to the FCSC would be deemed to have exited from the Service.
An officer whose service is no longer required would be required to leave the Service due to the following reasons except:
A Grade Level 17 Director in the Public Service who already spent............years on the post must compulsorily retire from the Service.
A Permanent Secretary should hold office for a term of........years and renewable for a further term of........years, subject to satisfactory performance.
Officers who exit the Service would be paid his retirement benefits and would access his National Housing Funds (NHF) contributions within.......of retirement.

A person engaged by the Government on a non-pensionable appointment basis is eligible for a.......... of his basic salary as a severance allowance after the satisfactory completion of a tour of service.
Interdiction means
option_a: Transition from old pension scheme to the new pension.
option_b: Forgiving arising from the gap in service.
option_c: Temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken or is about to be undertaken and shall be placed on a half-salary pending the detaermination of the case.
option_d: Permanent removal of an officer from the public servicd and he /she shall be placed on a half-salary pending the detaermination of the case.

question_text: ...............is a reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
Suspension is a___________________
option_a: a. Transition from old pension scheme to the new pension.
option_b: Permanent removal of an officer from the public servicd and he /she shall be placed on a half-salary pending the detaermination of the case.
option_c: Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.
option_d: Forgiving arising from the gap in the length of service of an officer.

question_text: _____________is a post provided for under the Personnel emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.
option_b: Duty post of Senior Mid-Wifery of a Government recognised Hospital.
option_c: Security post of an office in the Nigerian Army.
option_d: UN Permanent post for an expatriate.

question_text: Public Service of the Federation is.........................
option_a: Parastatals of the Government of the Federation.
option_b: The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 Section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).
option_d: All the Personal Assistants to the Accounting Officers of the Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs).
review_notes: Duplicate option label B found in source: "b. All staff of the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC) Plc.".
Option C is missing or could not be recovered safely from the source.

question_text: The implementation of virtual meetings/engagements in the Public Service is...........
option_a: to promote business activities among government workers as a way of earning secondary income.
option_b: to ensure that employees are able to respond to work demands remotely.
option_c: to combat cyber crimes among public servants in the country.
option_d: to generate online revenue to make more funds for various expenditures of government.

question_text: The Federal Civil Service Commission has delegated full disciplinary powers against erring Officers on Salary Grade Level (GL).................., to Permanent Secretaries and Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices

The Federal Civil Service Commission has delegated full power of dismissal against erring Officers on Salary Grade Level (GL).................., to Permanent Secretaries and Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices.

.....................consists of a series of omissions or incompetence, the cummulative effect of which shows that the Officer is not capable of discharging efficiently the duties of the office he holds.
Before the proceedings for the removal of an Officer for general inefficiency may have been instituted, he must have been warned on..........occasions in writing.
............is a specific act of wrong-doing or an improper behaviour which is inimical to the image of the service and which ca be investigated and proved.
............ can lead to termination and retirement.
All disciplinary procedures should not exceed....... in the case of Junior Staff, except where it is a criminal case.
All disciplinary procedures should not exceed....... in the case of Senior Staff, except where it is a criminal case.
...........is a specific act of very serious wrong-doing and an improper behaviour which is inimical to the image of the service and which ca be investigated and if proven, may lead to dismissal.
An interdicted Officer may be placed on..............pay pending the determination of his case
The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct is...........
An Officer in a serious financial embarrassment will be disqualified from............
option_b: promotion or acting in a higher appointment.
option_c: going on statutory annual or causal leave.

question_text: Any Officer seeking elective position must resign.............before the election.
Generally, serving officers are prohibited from accepting chieftaincy titles until after................
option_a: an alimony is paid to the Government
option_b: the attainment of the rank of full directorship in the Public Service.

question_text: An Officer is in a serious financial embarrassment if the aggregate sum of his unsecured debts and liabilities at any point in time exceed..............
option_a: the sum of three times his monthly emoluments.
option_b: the sum of his twelve months salary.
option_c: the total amount of six months of statutory allowances.
option_d: the sum of nine (9) months of his emoluments.

question_text: The following are kinds of disciplinary measures that may be taken against any erring public officer except.
An appeal from an aggrieved Officer must be handled and concluded within.............
An appeal/petition must carry the following except:
option_c: Signature and Address of the Appellant/Petitioner/ Complainant.
option_d: National Identity Number (NIN) of the Appellant/Petitioner/ Complainant.

question_text: An Appeal/ petition will not be looked into, for the following reasons except.
option_a: deals with s case in which legal action is pending in a COurt of Law.
option_c: complies with Public Service Rule (PSR) 110102 of the year, 2021.
option_d: is worded in abusive, improper, foul language or a repetition of a previous petition.

question_text: .............is the maximum aggregate sick leave that an officer is entitled to, during any period of twelve months.
When a pensionable officer dies in Service, the Government will pay.......percent of his total annual emolument to the deceased family as burial expenses.
Any prolonged absence from duty on account of illness should be supported with the following documents except:
For a medical check-up abroad, officers on........must obtain the approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
Where medical treatment is to occur in Nigeria but outside the Officer's duty post, he must be provided with.........at the prevailing rate.
...........is the authorized absence of an Officer from duty for specific period.

Thirty (30) working days, Twenty-one (21) working days and Fourteen (14) working days are authorized leave period for Officers on Grade Levels............,................ and................respectively.
option_a: GL 07 and above, GL 04-06 and GL 03 and below
option_b: GL 15 and above, GL 14-08 and GL 07 and below
option_c: GL 14 and above, GL 13-07 and GL 06 and below
option_d: GL 16 and above, GL 15-09 and GL 08 and below.

question_text: An Officer would qualify for annual leave not less than............. after the previous leave within a leave earning service year.
Leave will be taken at one time but it may be taken in not more than..............
Any leave not utilized within the............. concerned will be forfeited.
option_a: first three (3) months of the following year
option_c: initial two (2) months of the following year
option_d: first month of the subsequent year

question_text: An Officer will be granted a proportionate leave for the period he put in Service for the reasons except:
option_a: If he is attending a course of instruction/training over a period of six months.
option_b: If he is retiring within the period of Leave-Earning Service.
option_c: If he is joining the Federal Public Service during the course of the Leave Year.
option_d: If he is working with the Head of an Extra-Ministerial Office as a domestic servant.

question_text: .........is the maximum number of days of a casual leave that an officer is entitled to, after exhausting his annual leave.

If a female officer adopts a child under four months old with an evidence of approval of the adoption, she would enjoy.........maternity leave.
If a family of a male officer adopts a child under four months old with an evidence of approval of the adoption, he would enjoy.........maternity leave.
Sabbatical leave is applicable to Officers on GL 15 and above from duty for a period of..........calendar months once in...........years
Study leave in the public service includes the following except:

option_b: Presidential Special School Scheme for Independent Development (PRESSSID)
option_c: Presidential Special Scholarship Scheme for Innovation and Development (PRESSSID)
option_d: Presidential Safe School Scheme for Innovation and Development (PRESSSID)

question_text: The conditions for study leave without pay include the following except:
option_a: the proposed course of study is contained in the approved training proposal for the MDAs of the concerned officer
option_b: Officers on study leave without pay are entitled to normal emoluments and allowances.
option_c: the duration of the study leave without pay must not be more than four years in the first instance
option_d: the period of study should not be regarded as a break in service.

question_text: An Officer may be allowed special leave from duty on full pay on compassionate ground for a period up to.........for burial of spouse/child/parents/parents of spouse.
.........is absence of an Officer from duty authorized on grounds of public policy.
Leave of absence can be in form of the following except.

option_b: Leave of absence on public policy for Technical Aid programmes and Special / Personal Assistant on grounds of public policy.
option_c: Leave of absence for spouse of Presidents, Vice Presidents, Governors, Deputy Governors on grounds of public policy.
option_d: Leave of absence to take up political appointments such as Ministers, Commissioners, SSG, SGF.

question_text: A female officer who is nursing a child is entitled to........every day up to a maximum period of.........from the date she resumes duty from maternity leave.

option_b: A retiring officer with spouse and four (4) children
option_c: An officer undertaking responsibility using his own car and on transfer or posting or where air transport services do not exist
option_d: An officer working with a Chief Executive, living at a very far distance to the office.

option_b: Self, parents and domestic workers, Responsibility allowance

option_d: On a delegation or visit to an organization abroad for a short period.
